summaryAm: አንድ ንጉሥ እውነትን ከልጅ ድምፅ የሚማርበት ቀላል እና ቀልድ ያለው ታሪክ።

source.name: Hans Christian Andersen tale
source.notes: Public-domain source inspiration only. This is an original child-friendly Amharic adaptation with softened themes for ages 3-6.

pages.textAm: አንድ ንጉሥ ቀለማትን እና አዲስ ልብሶችን በጣም ይወድ ነበር።
pages.textEn: A king loved colors and new clothes very much.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Emperor's New Clothes, page 1: አንድ ንጉሥ ቀለማትን እና አዲስ ልብሶችን በጣም ይወድ ነበር።

pages.textAm: ሁለት ሰፊዎች በጣም ልዩ ልብስ እንደሚሠሩ ተናገሩ።
pages.textEn: Two tailors said they could make a very special outfit.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Emperor's New Clothes, page 2: ሁለት ሰፊዎች በጣም ልዩ ልብስ እንደሚሠሩ ተናገሩ።

pages.textAm: ሰዎች እውነትን ለመናገር ትንሽ ፈሩ፣ ግን ልባቸው ጥያቄ ነበረው።
pages.textEn: People were a little afraid to tell the truth, though their hearts had questions.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Emperor's New Clothes, page 3: ሰዎች እውነትን ለመናገር ትንሽ ፈሩ፣ ግን ልባቸው ጥያቄ ነበረው።

pages.textAm: በበዓል ቀን ንጉሡ በብዙ ቀለም ያለው ልብስ ወጣ፣ ሁሉም ግን ልዩ ልብሱ የት እንዳለ በጸጥታ ተመለከተ።
pages.textEn: On festival day, the king came out in colorful clothes while everyone quietly wondered about the special outfit.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Warm storybook illustration of a king in colorful festival clothes while townspeople quietly wonder about the promised special outfit.

pages.textAm: አንድ ልጅ በአደባባዩ ውስጥ ቆሞ “የማየውን በደግነት መናገር እችላለሁ?” ብሎ አሰበ።
pages.textEn: A child stood in the square and wondered if they could kindly say what they saw.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Emperor's New Clothes, page 5: a thoughtful child in a colorful town square wondering how to speak kindly.

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ የሰዎቹን ጸጥታ አየ። “ምናልባት እኔም በቀስታ መጠየቅ አለብኝ” ብሎ አሰበ።
pages.textEn: The king noticed the quiet crowd and wondered if he also needed to ask gently.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for The Emperor's New Clothes, page 6: a colorful king noticing a quiet crowd and thinking carefully.

pages.textAm: ሰፊዎቹ ባዶ እጆቻቸውን በቀስታ አውርደው ጸጥ አሉ። አንድ አገልጋይ በልቡ “እውነትን በደግነት መናገር ይቻላል” ብሎ አሰበ።
pages.textEn: The tailors slowly lowered their empty hands, and a helper thought that truth could be spoken kindly.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Two playful tailors lowering their empty hands while a palace helper looks thoughtful.

pages.textAm: ንጉሡ መስታወቱ ፊት ቆሞ ጸጥ ብሎ አሰበ። “እውነት መጠየቅ አያሳፍርም” ብሎ በልቡ ተናገረ።
pages.textEn: The king quietly wondered whether asking the truth could be brave.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Kind king standing thoughtfully before a mirror in a colorful dressing room.

pages.textAm: በከተማው አደባባይ ልጆች ቀለም ያላቸውን ጨርቆች አንጠለጠሉ። በዓሉ የሚያምር ነበር፣ ነገር ግን ሰዎች እውነትን በቀስታ መናገር ይፈልጉ ነበር።
pages.textEn: At the festival, everyone wanted to speak truth gently.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Town square festival with children hanging colorful cloth banners.

pages.textAm: ልጁ ቃሉን ከተናገረ በኋላ አደባባዩ ጸጥ አለ። ንጉሡም በቀስታ “እኔም በጥንቃቄ መስማት አለብኝ” አለ።
pages.textEn: After the child spoke, the king chose to listen carefully.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child speaking kindly in a festival crowd while the king listens.

pages.textAm: ከዚያ ቀን በኋላ ንጉሡ በቤተመንግሥቱ የእውነት ቀን አዘጋጀ። ሁሉም ሰው የሚያስበውን በደግነት መናገር ተማረ።
pages.textEn: After that day, the palace practiced kind truth-telling.
pages.illustrationPrompt: King and townspeople sharing colorful cloth in a cheerful palace courtyard.
